title: San Francisco Sourdough Boule

seo.title: How to Make San Francisco Sourdough Boule — Easy American Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to make authentic San Francisco Sourdough Boule. This easy American recipe includes step-by-step instructions, tips, and serving ideas.
longTailKeywords: how to make San Francisco Sourdough Boule
easy San Francisco Sourdough Boule recipe
authentic American San Francisco Sourdough Boule
lowCompetitionKeywords: American San Francisco Sourdough Boule recipe
seo.h1: Authentic San Francisco Sourdough Boule Recipe
h2Headings: What is San Francisco Sourdough Boule?
Pro Tips for the Best Results
seo.imageAlt: Authentic American San Francisco Sourdough Boule recipe
seo.imageCaption: Delicious homemade San Francisco Sourdough Boule — a beloved American classic

description: San Francisco Sourdough Boule is a masterpiece of American baking, celebrated for its distinct tangy flavor and wonderfully chewy crumb. This iconic bread, with its thick, crackly crust, embodies the culinary spirit of its namesake city. Perfect for sandwiches, toast, or simply slathered with butter, it's a true delight for any bread lover.

instructions.title: Mix the Dough
instructions.text: In a large mixing bowl, combine the active sourdough starter and warm water. Stir until the starter is mostly dissolved. Add the bread flour and whole wheat flour, mixing with a wooden spoon or your hands until no dry streaks of flour remain. Cover the bowl and let it rest for 30 minutes for the autolyse.

instructions.title: Add Salt and Begin Bulk Fermentation
instructions.text: After the autolyse, sprinkle the fine sea salt over the dough. Incorporate the salt by gently folding and squeezing the dough for 2-3 minutes until it feels smooth and the salt is fully mixed in. Lightly grease the bowl with olive oil, then cover and begin the bulk fermentation. Over the next 3-4 hours, perform 3-4 sets of stretch and folds every 30-45 minutes.

instructions.title: Pre-shape the Boule
instructions.text: After bulk fermentation, gently turn the dough out onto a lightly floured surface. Gently shape the dough into a loose round or rectangle, allowing it to rest for 20-30 minutes uncovered. This step helps the gluten relax and makes final shaping easier.

instructions.title: Final Shape and Proof
instructions.text: Lightly flour your hands and the dough. Gently shape the dough into a tight boule, creating tension on the surface. Transfer the shaped dough seam-side up into a well-floured banneton or a bowl lined with a floured cloth. Cover and refrigerate for 12-18 hours for a slow cold proof, which develops deeper flavor.

instructions.title: Preheat and Bake
instructions.text: Preheat your oven to 475F (245C) with a Dutch oven inside for at least 30 minutes. Carefully remove the hot Dutch oven. Invert the cold dough into the hot Dutch oven, score the top with a sharp blade or lame, and cover. Bake for 20 minutes with the lid on, then remove the lid.

instructions.title: Finish Baking and Cool
instructions.text: Reduce the oven temperature to 450F (230C) and continue baking for another 15-20 minutes, or until the crust is deeply golden brown and the internal temperature reaches 200-210F (93-99C). Carefully remove the bread from the Dutch oven and transfer it to a wire rack to cool completely for at least 2 hours before slicing. This allows the crumb to set.

tips: For an extra tangy sourdough, increase the cold proofing time to up to 24 hours in the refrigerator.
If you dont have a banneton, a colander lined with a well-floured tea towel works perfectly for proofing.
Always let your sourdough cool completely before slicing to ensure a perfect crumb texture and prevent gumminess.
article: San Francisco Sourdough Boule is a beloved American Baking dish enjoyed across American. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
